root_text.sn55.8:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā ñātike viharati giñjakāvasathe.
root_text.sn55.8:1.3: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn55.8:3.1: “Sāḷho, ānanda, bhikkhu kālaṅkato āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja vihāsi.
root_text.sn55.8:3.2: Nandā, ānanda, bhikkhunī kālaṅkatā pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā opapātikā tattha parinibbāyinī anāvattidhammā tasmā lokā.
root_text.sn55.8:3.3: Sudatto, ānanda, upāsako kālaṅkato tiṇṇaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā rāgadosamohānaṁ tanuttā sakadāgāmī; sakideva imaṁ lokaṁ āgantvā dukkhassantaṁ karissati.
root_text.sn55.8:3.4: Sujātā, ānanda, upāsikā kālaṅkatā tiṇṇaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā sotāpannā avinipātadhammā niyatā sambodhiparāyaṇā.
root_text.sn55.8:4.1: Anacchariyaṁ kho panetaṁ, ānanda, yaṁ manussabhūto kālaṁ kareyya;
root_text.sn55.8:4.2: tasmiṁ tasmiñce maṁ kālaṅkate upasaṅkamitvā etamatthaṁ paṭipucchissatha. Vihesā pesā, ānanda, assa tathāgatassa.
root_text.sn55.8:4.3: Tasmātihānanda, dhammādāsaṁ nāma dhammapariyāyaṁ desessāmi; yena samannāgato ariyasāvako ākaṅkhamāno attanāva attānaṁ byākareyya:
root_text.sn55.8:4.4: ‘khīṇanirayomhi khīṇatiracchānayoni khīṇapettivisayo khīṇāpāyaduggativinipāto, sotāpannohamasmi avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyaṇo’.
root_text.sn55.8:5.1: Katamo ca so, ānanda, dhammādāso dhammapariyāyo; yena samannāgato ariyasāvako ākaṅkhamāno attanāva attānaṁ byākareyya:
root_text.sn55.8:5.2: ‘khīṇanirayomhi khīṇatiracchānayoni khīṇapettivisayo khīṇāpāyaduggativinipāto, sotāpannohamasmi avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyaṇo’?
root_text.sn55.8:6.1: Idha, ānanda, ariyasāvako buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.8:6.4: ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgato hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn55.8:6.5: Ayaṁ kho so, ānanda, dhammādāso dhammapariyāyo;
root_text.sn55.8:6.6: yena samannāgato ariyasāvako ākaṅkhamāno attanāva attānaṁ byākareyya:
root_text.sn55.8:6.7: ‘khīṇanirayomhi khīṇatiracchānayoni khīṇapettivisayo khīṇāpāyaduggativinipāto, sotāpannohamasmi avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyaṇo’”ti.

translation_text.sn55.8:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying at Ñātika in the brick house.
translation_text.sn55.8:1.3: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn55.8:2.1: “Sir, the monk named Sāḷha has passed away.
translation_text.sn55.8:2.2: Where has he been reborn in his next life?
translation_text.sn55.8:2.7: and the laywoman named Sujātā have passed away.
translation_text.sn55.8:2.8: Where have they been reborn in the next life?”
translation_text.sn55.8:3.1: “Ānanda, the monk Sāḷha passed away having realized the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life, having realized it with his own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.sn55.8:3.2: The nun Nandā passed away having ended the five lower fetters. She’s been reborn spontaneously, and will be extinguished there, not liable to return from that world.
translation_text.sn55.8:3.3: The layman Sudatta passed away having ended three fetters, and weakened greed, hate, and delusion. He’s a once-returner; he will come back to this world once only, then make an end of suffering.
translation_text.sn55.8:3.4: The laywoman Sujātā passed away having ended three fetters. She’s a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.
translation_text.sn55.8:4.1: It’s no wonder that a human being should pass away.
translation_text.sn55.8:4.2: But if you should come and ask me about it each and every time someone dies that would be a bother for me.
translation_text.sn55.8:4.3: So Ānanda, I will teach you the explanation of the Dhamma named ‘the mirror of the teaching’. A noble disciple who has this may declare of themselves:
translation_text.sn55.8:4.4: ‘I’ve finished with rebirth in hell, the animal realm, and the ghost realm. I’ve finished with all places of loss, bad places, the underworld. I am a stream-enterer! I’m not liable to be reborn in the underworld, and am destined for awakening.’
translation_text.sn55.8:5.1: And what is that mirror of the teaching?
translation_text.sn55.8:6.1: It’s when a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.8:6.4: And they have the ethical conduct loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.
translation_text.sn55.8:6.5: This is that mirror of the teaching.
translation_text.sn55.8:6.6: A noble disciple who has this may declare of themselves:
translation_text.sn55.8:6.7: ‘I’ve finished with rebirth in hell, the animal realm, and the ghost realm. I’ve finished with all places of loss, bad places, the underworld. I am a stream-enterer! I’m not liable to be reborn in the underworld, and am destined for awakening.’”
translation_text.sn55.8:6.9: (The following two discourses have the same setting.)
